description_long: This is a heavy-duty mallet-style hammer with a large, cylindrical head made of a dark, dense material, likely metal or hard rubber. The head is solid and symmetrical, with flat striking faces on both ends, suitable for delivering forceful, non-marring blows. The handle is straight with a slight ergonomic contour, covered in a dark, textured grip material that appears to be rubber or a similar synthetic for comfort and slip resistance. The transition between the head and handle is seamless, indicating a robust construction designed for durability. The overall appearance suggests it is intended for tasks requiring controlled, powerful impacts, such as driving stakes, assembling parts, or working with masonry. There are no visible brand markings, damage, or additional attachments on the hammer.
description: A robust mallet-style hammer with a large cylindrical head and a dark, ergonomic rubberized handle, designed for heavy-duty striking tasks.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen from the top, showing the rounded end of the cylindrical head and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the full cylindrical head, flat striking faces, and the straight, textured handle.
description_view_2: The bottom view displays the opposite end of the head and the handle's ergonomic grip.
description_view_3: Another side view, nearly identical to view 1, showing the cylindrical head and handle profile.
attribute_handle_type: Rubberized ergonomic grip handle, straight with a slight contour for comfort and slip resistance.
attribute_head_type: Large cylindrical mallet head with flat striking faces on both ends, likely made of metal or hard rubber.
